originalTitle: Nigerian police arrest 15 after viral videos of alleged sexual assault at festival
title: Police Heroes Keep Everyone Safe in Nigeria!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hello, super friends! It's your friendly African news reporter here, ready to share some important news with you! My name is Reporter Rina, and I love sharing stories about our amazing continent. Today, we're going to talk about how good people, especially our brave police officers, help keep us all safe and happy in our wonderful country, Nigeria! Remember, even when sad things happen, there are always good people helping to make things right.

What is a Festival?

Have you ever been to a big party or a special gathering with lots of people, music, and delicious food? We call those 'festivals'! They are super fun times where many people come together to sing, dance, and celebrate! In Nigeria, just like in many other places in Africa, we love our festivals. They are full of bright colours, happy smiles, and lots of joy. People gather to celebrate special things, like a good harvest or a special holiday. It's a time for friendship and happiness! **Did you know?** Nigeria is a very big country in West Africa! It's famous for its vibrant music, amazing movies, and tasty jollof rice! There are so many different cultures and languages there.

When Things Go Wrong at a Happy Place

Sometimes, even at a happy festival, sad things can happen. Recently, at a big festival in Nigeria, some people were not kind to others. They made some people feel very scared and sad. This is not okay at all. Everyone, especially girls and women, deserves to feel safe and happy all the time, everywhere they go. No one should ever make another person feel scared or upset.

What Does 'Safe' Mean?

Being safe means feeling happy and protected. It means no one should ever hurt you, or make you feel scared, or touch you in a way you don't like. Your body is special, and it belongs only to you! If anyone ever makes you feel unsafe, or tries to touch you in a way you don't like, it's super important to tell a trusted grown-up right away. This could be your mom, dad, a teacher, a grandparent, or another family member. They are always there to help you!

Police Officers to the Rescue!

When bad things happen, who helps us? Our wonderful police officers! Police officers are like superheroes in our communities. Their job is to keep everyone safe and make sure people follow the rules. They wear special uniforms and often drive special cars. They are very brave and strong, and they work hard every day to protect us all. **Did you know?** In Nigeria, police cars often have blue and white colours and special lights that flash! These lights tell everyone that the police are on their way to help.

Police Caught the People Who Were Not Kind

In this story, when the police heard about people being unkind and making others feel unsafe at the festival, they quickly went to work! They listened carefully and found out who was doing these bad things. Guess what? The police arrested 15 people! 'Arrested' means they caught these people and took them to the police station. This is so important because it means the police are making sure that people who do bad things learn that it's not okay. It helps make sure they won't hurt others again, and it keeps our communities safer for everyone.

Many People Were Sad and Spoke Up

When many people heard about what happened at the festival, they felt very, very sad and also a little bit angry. They wanted to make sure that everyone understood how important it is to be kind and respectful to girls and women, and to absolutely everyone! They wanted to say, 'Stop hurting people! Everyone deserves respect and to feel safe!' It's good when many people stand together to say what is right.

Sending Special Messages Online

How did people share their feelings? Many people used their phones and computers to send special messages to everyone! They used something called a 'hashtag.' Imagine it like a special sign on the internet that says 'Hey, listen to this important message!' Many people used a hashtag that meant 'Let's make sure girls and women are safe and treated kindly!' It was a way for many voices to join together and say, 'We want kindness and safety for all!' **Did you know?** A 'hashtag' always starts with a '#' symbol, like #HappyKids or #BeKind! It helps people find messages about the same topic online.

Why It Matters to Be Kind

This story reminds us all how important it is to be kind and respectful to everyone, all the time. Every single person, whether they are a boy or a girl, big or small, old or young, deserves to be treated with love and respect. We should always make others feel safe and happy, never scared or sad. Being kind makes our hearts happy and our world a better place.

What Can We Do to Help?

So, what can YOU do to help make our community and our world a safer and happier place? Here are some ideas:

1. **Be a good friend:** Always play nicely, share your toys, and use kind words with your friends and family.

2. **Use kind words:** Say nice things to people. A kind word can make someone's whole day brighter!

3. **Respect everyone's body:** Remember that everyone's body is special and belongs only to them. We should never touch someone if they don't want us to, or in a way that makes them uncomfortable. Always ask first!

4. **Tell a trusted grown-up:** If you ever see someone being unkind, or if someone makes you feel unsafe, remember to tell a trusted adult right away. They will listen to you and help you! Speaking up is very brave.

Africa, Our Amazing Home

Our home, Africa, is a continent full of amazing people, beautiful cultures, and vibrant communities. We all want to make sure that every part of Africa is a safe and happy place for everyone, especially for all the wonderful children like you! When we work together, we can make our communities even better and stronger. **Did you know?** Africa is the second-largest continent in the world! It has 54 different countries, each with its own special traditions and stories. How cool is that?

A Big Thank You to Our Police!

Let's give a big 'thank you' to the police officers in Nigeria who worked hard to catch the people who were not kind. They showed us that good people are always working to keep us safe and make sure rules are followed. We are lucky to have them protecting our communities!

Let's Keep Our World Happy and Safe!

Remember, little stars, being kind, staying safe, and telling a grown-up when something is wrong are super important. You are brave and strong, and your voice matters. Let's all help each other live in a safe and happy world! That's all for today from your friendly African news reporter. See you next time!
summary: Police in Nigeria helped keep people safe by catching some individuals who were unkind at a big festival, and many people shared messages online to say everyone deserves to be treated kindly and feel safe.
whyMatters: This is important because everyone, especially girls and women, should always feel safe and happy wherever they are. Police help protect us, and we can all help too by being kind, respectful, and telling a trusted grown-up if something makes us feel unsafe or sad.

originalTitle: UN expert says world has given Israel ‘licence to torture Palestinians’
title: Our UN Friend Says: Everyone Deserves Kindness!
content:
Hello, super friends! It’s your friendly news reporter from Africa, here to tell you about something very important. Just like in your homes and schools, we all want to be treated with kindness and fairness, right? We want to feel safe and happy. But sometimes, in the big wide world, things don't always work that way for everyone.

Meet Our Special Helper, Ms. Francesca

There's a very special grown-up named Ms. Francesca. She works for a really big and important group called the United Nations, or UN for short. Imagine the UN like a huge, friendly team made up of almost all the countries in the world! Their main job is to help people everywhere live in peace, be safe, and be happy. Ms. Francesca is like a special detective or a super helper for the UN. Her job is to look very, very closely at how people are treated in different places around the world. She travels and talks to people, always trying to make sure everyone is safe and treated with kindness, no matter where they live or who they are.

Did you know?

**Did you know?** The United Nations (UN) was started a long, long time ago after a big world war. Its main goal is to keep peace and help countries work together like a big family!

A Problem in a Faraway Land

Ms. Francesca has been looking at a place far, far away from us. In this place, there are two groups of people who live close to each other. One group is called the Palestinians, and the other group lives in a country called Israel. They share the same land, but sometimes they have big problems. Ms. Francesca says something very worrying. She says that some people, especially the Palestinians, are being treated very, very badly. Imagine if some grown-ups in charge were letting people get hurt, or making them feel very scared and sad, even when they shouldn't be. That's a very unkind way to treat someone. This kind of very bad treatment, where people are hurt or made to suffer badly, is sometimes called 'torture'. Ms. Francesca says this unkind treatment is happening. She believes it's become a 'way of doing things' for some of the grown-ups in charge in Israel, which is not fair at all.

Did you know?

**Did you know?** There's a special rulebook for the whole world called the 'Universal Declaration of Human Rights'. It says that everyone in the world has rights, like the right to be safe, the right to go to school, and the right to be treated fairly!

Why Does This Matter to Everyone?

Ms. Francesca also said something very important about why this problem keeps happening. She thinks that because other big countries and grown-ups around the world haven't said 'STOP!' loud enough, it's like they are giving permission for this bad treatment to continue. It's like they are letting it happen. Imagine if a bully was bothering someone in the playground, and all the other grown-ups just watched and didn't say anything. That might make the bully think it's okay to keep going, right? Ms. Francesca feels that's similar to what's happening now. She wants everyone to speak up.

Did you know?

**Did you know?** The Middle East is a very old and special part of our world. It has amazing history, beautiful places, and many different kinds of people who have lived there for a very, very long time.

Everyone Deserves Kindness

Why does this matter so much? Because every single person in the world, no matter where they live, what colour their skin is, or what language they speak, deserves to be safe. Every child, every grown-up, everyone deserves to be treated with respect and kindness. No one should ever be made to feel scared or hurt by others. And certainly, no one should be treated badly by people who are supposed to protect them and keep them safe. It's just not fair, and it's not right.

What Can We Do?

Even though we are far away, we can still think about how important kindness is. We can remember to treat our friends, family, and everyone we meet with fairness and respect. We can also learn about what is happening in the world and hope for peace. Ms. Francesca and the UN are working hard to make sure everyone is treated well. They are asking all the grown-ups in charge, and all the countries, to remember their promise to keep people safe and to speak up when things are unfair.

Did you know?

**Did you know?** People all over the world, including many children like you, hope for a future where everyone can live in peace and happiness. It’s a dream we can all share! So, let’s all remember the lesson from Ms. Francesca: kindness is for everyone, everywhere. And when we see something unfair, it’s important to speak up and help. Together, we can hope for a world where every single person feels safe, happy, and treated with love. That's all for today from your friendly news reporter. Keep spreading kindness, friends!
summary: A special helper from the United Nations named Ms. Francesca says that people called Palestinians in a faraway place are being treated very unkindly, and she wants countries around the world to speak up and help stop it.
whyMatters: This is important because every person, no matter where they live, deserves to be safe and treated with kindness and fairness, and we must all try to help when people are hurting or being treated badly.
